report_name: New 'OXLOADER' Malware Uses Malicious Google Ads to Distribute CastleStealer

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: The attack chain begins with a user clicking a malicious Google Ad that leads to a fraudulent website.

attack_patterns.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
attack_patterns.description: The fraudulent website prompts the user to download a batch script hosted on Storj, a decentralized cloud storage platform, to initiate the infection.

attack_patterns.name: Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism: Bypass User Account Control
attack_patterns.description: The initial script triggers a User Account Control (UAC) prompt to execute the main OXLOADER payload with elevated privileges.

attack_patterns.name: Execution Guardrails: Environmental Keying
attack_patterns.description: OXLOADER performs checks to determine the system's location and terminates if it is within the Commonwealth of Independent States (CIS) region.

attack_patterns.name: Credentials from Password Stores
attack_patterns.description: The final payload, CastleStealer, is designed to steal credentials from browsers, email clients, and cryptocurrency wallets.

attack_patterns.name: Obfuscated Files or Information
attack_patterns.description: OXLOADER is heavily obfuscated, modifying its own code in memory to decrypt the next stage and abusing the .reloc section to stage shellcode.

attack_patterns.name: Windows Command Shell
attack_patterns.description: A batch script is downloaded and executed by the user to start the infection chain.

attack_patterns.name: PowerShell
attack_patterns.description: Hunting hints suggest looking for PowerShell commands with '-ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File' used to download and execute scripts from untrusted sources.

malware.name: OXLOADER
malware.description: A new, sophisticated malware loader distributed via Google Ads. It uses advanced evasion techniques, including self-modifying code and geofencing, to deploy a final payload.

malware.name: CastleStealer
malware.description: A .NET-based information stealer delivered by OXLOADER. It is designed to steal credentials from browsers, email clients, and cryptocurrency wallets.

campaigns.name: REF8372
campaigns.description: A malvertising campaign that abuses Google Ads to distribute the OXLOADER malware, which in turn deploys the CastleStealer infostealer. The campaign targets users searching for legitimate software like 'node.js'.
campaigns.objective: Distribute infostealer malware for financial gain.

infrastructure.name: Malicious Node.js Download Site
infrastructure.description: A fraudulent website at node-js.prentiva99.info, promoted via Google Ads, that tricks users into downloading a malicious script.

infrastructure.name: Storj Decentralized Storage
infrastructure.description: The attackers used Storj, a decentralized cloud storage platform, to host the initial batch script, helping to evade domain-based reputation filters and takedowns.

indicators.name: OXLOADER Malicious and Staging Domain DNS Query
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: OXLOADER Malicious and Staging Domain DNS Query
id: 5b9e1a2f-3c4d-4e5f-8a9b-0c1d2e3f4a5b
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries to domains associated with the OXLOADER malvertising campaign, including the fraudulent download site and the decentralized storage gateway.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/new-oxloader-loader-uses-malicious.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-22
tags:
  - attack.t1105
  - attack.command_and_control
level: high
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName|contains:
      - 'node-js.prentiva99.info'
      - 'gateway.storjshare.io'
  condition: selection

indicators.name: Suspicious PowerShell Execution Policy Bypass
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious PowerShell Execution Policy Bypass For File Execution
id: 6c8d7b6a-5e4f-4d3c-b2a1-9e8f7d6c5b4a
status: experimental
description: Detects PowerShell being launched with the ExecutionPolicy Bypass flag to run a script file, a technique often used by malware to run untrusted code.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/new-oxloader-loader-uses-malicious.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-22
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.execution
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'powershell'
      - '-ExecutionPolicy Bypass'
      - '-File'
  condition: selection

indicators.name: Suspicious Execution from User Temp Directory
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Execution from User Temp Directory
id: 7d6c5b4a-8e7f-4c3b-a19e-8f7d6c5b4a9e
status: experimental
description: Detects processes being executed from common user temporary or application data directories, which is a common tactic for malware droppers like OXLOADER.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/new-oxloader-loader-uses-malicious.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-22
tags:
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.execution
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|contains:
      - ':\Users\Public\'
      - '\AppData\Local\Temp\'
      - '\AppData\Roaming\'
  filter_legit:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\Updater.exe'
      - '\setup.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit

indicators.name: Suspicious Modification of Registry Run Key
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Modification of Registry Run Key
id: 8e7f6d5c-9f8e-4b2a-9e8f-7d6c5b4a9e8f
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation or modification of values in the user's Run key, a common persistence technique used by malware.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/new-oxloader-loader-uses-malicious.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-22
tags:
  - attack.t1547.001
  - attack.persistence
level: medium
logsource:
  category: registry_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetObject|contains: '\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run'
  condition: selection

relationships.target_name: Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism: Bypass User Account Control
